blockers.title: Guardian consent expiry is not audited
blockers.evidence: Stage 0 requires every member-affecting write to emit AuditLog evidence. This slice changes `GuardianConsent.confirmation_state` to `expired` with no `writeAudit` in both expiry paths: scheduled expiry at `convex/guardians.ts:266-279` and expired-token confirm at `convex/guardians.ts:442-450`. For a minor consent gate, losing the expiry/refusal history is a broken safeguarding audit trail.
blockers.required_fix: Write a no-PII AuditLog row whenever a guardian token is marked expired, from both the scheduled marker and the confirm-time fallback. Add Convex tests asserting the expiry audit row.

blockers.title: Guardian email copy is not verbatim from the vault
blockers.evidence: AC1/AC6 require guardian-facing strings to use the vault draft verbatim. The vault line is `To give your consent, please click below...` in `01 Under-18 Launch Copy (Drafts).md:50-54`, but `convex/lib/guardianEmail.ts:34-39` changes the sentence to `please open the link below and press the confirmation button` and splits the safeguarding/privacy sentence. The file comment claims `VERBATIM`, but the shipped string is not.
blockers.required_fix: Use the exact vault wording with only approved placeholder substitutions/link URLs, or update the vault/spec first and cite that change.

blockers.title: Committed Gate 4 review artifact is stale
blockers.evidence: `.codex/audits/guardian-consent.review.json:1` is added in this diff and still records old DO-NOT-SHIP findings that current code has already changed: no-JS consent-by-email copy, rollback missing `prevState`, and resend quota burn. Merging that file leaves a false audit trail for this slice.
blockers.required_fix: Regenerate or replace `.codex/audits/guardian-consent.review.json` with the current Gate 4 verdict, or move failed rounds to round-specific filenames before merge.

non_blocking_notes: Targeted secret scan found no committed secrets; matches were env-var names and test dummy values.
`npm test -- --run tests/unit/guardianToken.test.ts tests/convex/guardianConsent.test.ts` could not start in this read-only sandbox because Vite tried to write `node_modules/.vite-temp/...`.
`npm run build` and `npx astro check` could not run because Astro tried to write `.astro/content.d.ts`.
`npx convex codegen --typecheck=disable` could not complete under restricted network; it failed reporting to Sentry after `fetch failed`.

spec_compliance.criterion: AC1 guardian email sent at the right moment with vault copy
spec_compliance.evidence: The auth hook schedules `internal.guardians.sendGuardianEmail` when a minor reaches `pending_guardian`, but the email body in `convex/lib/guardianEmail.ts` is not verbatim from the vault draft.
Confirmation is a public mutation, not GET; lookup is neutral for invalid/expired/used tokens; token hashes are stored; expiry is enforced and scheduled.

spec_compliance.criterion: AC3 confirmation activates member, certificate, and audit
spec_compliance.evidence: `confirmGuardianConsent` patches the consent proof, member age/lifecycle fields, writes confirmation/lifecycle audit rows, and issues the certificate in one mutation.
Member resend is auth-derived, server-throttled with 1/hour and 3/day buckets, checks the shared global send cap, rotates tokens, and rolls back failed sends.
Active `minor` members receive the Aviation for Girls dashboard branch plus certificate section, with adult tiles/settings bypassed.
No em dash was found in added guardian strings, but the guardian email text diverges from the vault despite the verbatim requirement.
No at-18 graduation workflow, guardian revocation portal, event/photo reconfirmation, or admin queue was added.
Tests cover the main token/resend paths, but they do not catch the missing audit row on expiry or the non-verbatim guardian email copy.

summary: Do not ship. The main token flow is close, but this is a minors consent gate: expiry needs an auditable trail, guardian copy must match the vault, and the committed review artifact must not preserve stale false findings.
